title: kAPR: A coverage-guided, context-aware agent for automated repair of Linux kernel bugs
data:
B Li, X Yin, Y Zhang, S Liu, S Ji- Information and Software Technology, 2026
Context: With the widespread success of fuzzing, numerous bugs have been 
discovered in the Linux kernel, creating a backlog of unresolved bugs that 
underscores the need for automated program repair (APR). Large language models 
(LLMs) have recently emerged as a powerful paradigm for APR, achieving 
remarkable performance. However, unlike user-space programs, the Linux kernel 
presents unique challenges due to its massive scale and monolithic structure
Trch dn: Leveraging large language model for automatic patch correctness
author: Thanh Le-Cong

title: Leveraging Large Language Models for advanced static code analysis: Assessing the feasibility of AI superseding traditional vulnerability detection methods
data:
D Koterba, M ukaczyk, W Ksiek- Information and Software Technology, 2026
Context: Due to limited contextual understanding, traditional Static Application 
Security Testing (SAST) tools often struggle to detect complex software 
vulnerabilities, especially in dynamically typed languages. Large Language Models 
(LLMs), with their advanced contextual comprehension and language-agnostic 
nature, offer a compelling alternative to conventional security analysis methods. 
Objectives: This study aims to evaluate the effectiveness of contemporary LLMs as

title: Detecting AI-Generated Go Language Code via Supervised Learning on Task-Conditioned Entropy Sequences
data:
G Liaoa, L Chaia, Y Houa, Q Yuea- Procedia Computer Science, 2026
With the widespread application of artificial intelligence (AI) in code generation tasks, 
issues such as the quality, security, and intellectual property compliance of 
generated code have become increasingly prominent, necessitating the 
establishment of an effective automated AI-generated code detection mechanism.

attention has been paid to Go, a programming language widely used in cloud-native
